architecture.summary: The system trains a sparse autoencoder (SAE) on hidden activations from ESM-2 8M (a transformer-based protein language model). The SAE has an overcomplete bottleneck (10× expansion factor) with L1 sparsity regularization to disentangle polysemantic features. Latent components are interpreted via statistical association with protein annotations (precision/recall against UniProt labels). Selected latents are then used to steer sequence generation by scaling/shifting their activations in the residual stream during iterative inference, with reconstructed embeddings injected back before the decoder.

biblio.title: Interpreting and Steering Protein Language Models through Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: Sparse autoencoders identify latent components associated with various protein characteristics, including transmembrane regions, binding sites, and specialized motifs such as zinc finger domains
395 putative latent-feature associations are identified with minimum precision or recall of 0.8 at activation threshold 1.00
Intervening on the two most prominent latents (highest recall) associated with zinc finger regions produces 24 matching zinc finger motifs out of 180 generated sequences
claims.unit: sequences with detected zinc finger motifs

baseline.name: random latent or random pair intervention

claims.statement: Intervening on a single zinc finger latent produces only 3 matches out of 180 sequences, whereas random latent intervention or random latent pair intervention produces zero matches
The selected sparse autoencoder achieves an average of 18 non-zero latent components per amino acid token with a cross-entropy increase of 0.10
claims.unit: average active latents per token; CE increase 0.10

claims.statement: Layer selection is guided by intrinsic dimension analysis, identifying layer 3 of ESM-2 8M as a plateau region where abstract information is most clearly encoded

claims.dataset: generated sequences with zinc finger motifs
claims.statement: Among 24 matched zinc finger sequences, the highest percent sequence similarity was unreported, with an average sequence similarity of unreported, indicating a good level of diversity among generated sequences
The sequence generation pipeline requires parameter fine-tuning to improve the success rate; the process is not yet fully automated
